★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.59 3.37 4.11 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.76 0.59 0.87 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.79 0.75 0.91 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.04 2.03 2.34 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.55 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.07 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2023-05-16 47 35 23 284 2 426
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2019-03-14 18 8 10 299 1 299
Total weighted health score
394.3
lower is better

Deficiencies (79)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

76 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
2 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0577 Allow residents to easily view the nursing home's survey results and communicate with advocate agencies. D 2025-11-04 2025-12-29
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2025-11-04 2025-12-29
0627 Ensure the transfer/discharge meets the resident's needs/preferences and that the resident is prepared for a safe transfer/discharge. D 2025-11-04 2025-12-29
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. D 2025-06-05 2025-08-12
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2025-06-05 2025-08-12
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-06-05 2025-08-12
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2025-06-05 2025-09-19
0628 Provide the required documentation or notification related to the resident's needs, appeal rights, or bed-hold policies. D 2025-06-05 2025-09-19
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-06-05 2025-08-12
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2025-06-05 2025-08-12
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-06-05 2025-08-12
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-06-05 2025-09-19
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2024-12-06 2025-02-19
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. L 2024-12-06 2025-02-06
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. F 2024-12-06 2025-02-06
0838 Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. E 2024-12-06 2025-02-06
0843 Have an agreement with at least one or more hospitals certified by Medicare or Medicaid to make sure residents can be moved quickly to the hospital when they need medical care. F 2024-12-06 2025-02-06
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0577 Allow residents to easily view the nursing home's survey results and communicate with advocate agencies. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. E 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0685 Assist a resident in gaining access to vision and hearing services. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. E 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0710 Obtain a doctor's order to admit a resident and ensure the resident is under a doctor's care. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0791 Provide or obtain dental services for each resident. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0805 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food prepared in a form designed to meet individual needs. E 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. E 2023-05-16 2023-07-30
0917 Make sure each resident has 1) at least one window to the outside in a room; 2) a room at or above ground level; 3) adequate bedding; 4) furniture that meets the resident's needs; or 5) adequate closet space. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0924 Put firmly secured handrails on each side of hallways. E 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0551 Give the resident's representative the ability to exercise the resident's rights. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. F 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. E 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. E 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. E 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-30
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. F 2023-05-16 2023-08-28
